turningValues.scene: Marcus accepts the Chief Architect role

stakes.description: Marcus's desire to remain anonymous and avoid corporate stress
The loss of twelve hours of data for three million users
The collapse of the company's infrastructure and the livelihoods of the engineering team

protagonistDesires.goal: Maintain a low-stress, anonymous life as an L3 engineer
Maintain control and save the company's reputation
Secure the best talent to fix the failing backend

themeDeliveries.theme: True expertise cannot hide from responsibility
themeDeliveries.location: Marcus's internal struggle vs his inability to watch bad code destroy the system
The contrast between the panicked, incompetent management and Marcus's calm, effective solution

conflicts.description: Marcus's desire for peace vs his professional integrity and compulsion to fix broken systems
Marcus vs Richard Sterling regarding authority and technical competence
The engineering team vs the failing monolithic database architecture

premiseCounterPremise.counterPremise: Corporate hierarchy and titles dictate power
